edges.candidate: Export when the table has zero records: does the file contain only the header row, an empty file, or is the download suppressed? Also single-record export and a record whose every field is null/empty.

When no sort is specified or multiple records share the same sort-key value, is the row order in the CSV deterministic and stable across repeated exports, or arbitrary (e.g. DB insertion vs. current view order)?

A field value contains the delimiter (comma), a double-quote, a newline/CRLF, a leading '=' or '+' (CSV injection), or non-ASCII/multibyte characters (e.g. é, emoji): is it RFC-4180 quoted/escaped, what character encoding is written, and is a UTF-8 BOM emitted so Excel opens it correctly?

Records are inserted, updated, or deleted while the export is being generated/streamed: is the snapshot consistent (point-in-time) or can the CSV contain torn/partial rows or duplicates? Also two concurrent exports by the same user writing to the same temp/download target.
